name: Financials & banking
description: Bank credit, loans, delinquencies, money supply, financial conditions, and volatility for banks and insurers.

series.note: Aggregate loans plus securities held by U.S. commercial banks (H.8), the broadest gauge of bank balance-sheet expansion and credit supply to the economy.
C&I loan balances are the core barometer of business borrowing demand and bank lending appetite, leading capex and corporate-credit cycles.
Higher-frequency weekly C&I loan series (H.8) for near-real-time tracking of corporate borrowing momentum and credit tightening.
Bank real-estate lending (residential + CRE) drives a large share of loan books and is the front line of CRE-stress and mortgage-cycle risk.
Bank-held consumer credit balances signal household borrowing demand and consumer-lending growth across cards, auto, and personal loans.
Total deposit base is banks' primary low-cost funding; deposit growth or flight directly drives funding costs, liquidity, and net interest margin.
Total loans-and-leases (ex-securities) isolates pure lending activity, the revenue engine and credit-risk core of bank balance sheets.
Business-loan (C&I) delinquencies track deteriorating corporate credit quality, driving commercial provisioning and recession risk premia.
Residential mortgage delinquencies gauge housing-credit stress and loss content in bank mortgage and RMBS exposure.
Net charge-off rate is the realized-loss figure that directly hits card-issuer earnings and reserve adequacy, complementing delinquency leads.
Realized C&I loan losses quantify corporate-credit damage and drive commercial-bank provisioning through the cycle.
Aggregate NIM is the cleanest proxy for bank spread profitability, capturing the joint effect of rate levels, curve shape, and funding costs.
Bank reserves measure systemic liquidity created/drained by QE/QT; falling reserves tighten funding markets and pressure short-rate plumbing.
Comprehensive 100+ indicator gauge of financial-system tightness; positive = tighter-than-average conditions, a leading risk/credit-stress signal.
NFCI stripped of the economic cycle, isolating financial conditions that are unusually loose/tight given growth and inflation.
Composite of 18 yield/spread/volatility series; spikes flag systemic financial stress and risk-off regimes for the financial complex.
Equity-market implied volatility is the benchmark risk-appetite/fear gauge driving trading revenue, AUM flows, and credit-spread direction.
Short-term financial CP funding rate reveals money-market stress and the marginal cost of wholesale funding for banks and finance companies.
